repo_prior_art.cite: pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1: coordinatewise CQTE envelope certificates, not fixed-policy MSM-CVaR welfare

published_prior_art.cite: HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2025SharpOPE gives sharp mean MSM OPE; FanQiXu2025AlphaExpectedWelfare gives alpha welfare under conventional identification

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1: pointwise QTE envelope/certificate problem, not CVaR non-commutation against a monotone CDF band

published_prior_art.cite: DornGuo2021QuantileBalancing and MastenPoirierRen2025RelaxingUnconfoundedness give sharp pointwise/distributional sensitivity bounds, not the proposed CVaR band-relaxation gap

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_bound_intersection_inference_v1 is only a failed generic interval-intersection shell; no eta-indexed MSM-CVaR endpoint score is catalogued

published_prior_art.cite: DornGuoKallus2021DoublySharp gives orthogonal sharp MSM endpoint scores for ATE endpoints; ImbensManski2004PartialIDCI gives scalar interval coverage
RockafellarUryasev2000CVaR gives the variational CVaR identity; Gamma=1 only identifies the policy-induced distribution before applying it

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist omits JinXuZhengZhou2025, a closer 2025 MSM robust policy-learning comparator that should be distinguished from the proposed CVaR endpoint.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The unconfoundedness reduction is already the Rockafellar-Uryasev CVaR variational identity applied after Gamma=1 identification, so it should not be a §8 novelty result.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Sharpness of the outer CVaR endpoint is assumed open until a minimax or common-law pasting argument is supplied.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Conditional proposal claim: nested sharp MSM-CVaR endpoint only if the eta-indexed sharp conditional laws can be promoted to a valid full-data sharpness argument.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 3
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The one-step CLT is restricted to a unique robust quantile and positive density near active cutoffs.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Smooth asymptotic-linear inference only under Assumptions 4-6; mass-point cases are outside this proposal version.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 1 / Conjecture 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Multi-action latent compatibility beyond the actionwise MSM operator is not claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The proposal is bound to the actionwise-separable MSM convention; any coupled-action compatibility gap must be handled by restriction or a separate result.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add JinXuZhengZhou2025 to §5 and §4 Paragraph 2 as the closest robust policy-learning-under-MSM comparator, then state explicitly that it is mean or worst-case-welfare OPE rather than fixed-policy CVaR partial identification.
Move Theorem 1 out of §8 into §9 as a named sanity corollary, or keep it in §8 only with an explicit no-novelty calibration label and revise its §4 Gap clause.
For Conjecture 2, include a finite-support witness table with actual probabilities, weights, eta candidates, and computed Delta_nc to make the non-commutation gap auditable before Stage 0.

Pin the multi-action convention: either restrict the locked kernel to binary action or add an explicit finite-action latent-compatibility assumption that justifies simultaneous use of the actionwise MSM tilted operators.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: patch the missing comparator and demote or relabel the already-known Gamma=1 theorem, then advance to Stage 0 if no closer CVaR-under-MSM prior art appears.
